differential_diagnoses: Inflammatory Bowel Disease (Crohn's disease with extraintestinal manifestations)
next_steps: Perform punch biopsy of the advancing edge of an ulcer for histopathology and tissue culture
Consult dermatology for evaluation of pathergy-prone skin lesions
Initiate high-dose systemic corticosteroids after ruling out deep-seated infection via imaging/cultures
Perform colonoscopy to assess for underlying Crohn's disease given family history and clinical presentation
Assess serum inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP, ferritin)
Perform Pathergy test or evaluate for clinical signs of pathergy at trauma sites

differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Suppurative Hidradenitis)
Sweet syndrome (associated with potential underlying malignancy)
Septic emboli or occult deep-tissue infection (e.g., mycobacterial or fungal)
next_steps: Perform punch biopsy of the advancing ulcer edge (with deep tissue samples for tissue culture/PCR to rule out atypical infections)
Consult dermatology for formal assessment of pathergic lesions
Initiate systemic corticosteroids (e.g., high-dose IV methylprednisolone) as primary treatment for neutrophilic dermatosis after ruling out infection via biopsy
Perform bone marrow biopsy and peripheral blood smear to rule out underlying hematologic malignancy
Genetic testing for PSTPIP1 mutations to confirm PASH syndrome
Monitor response of intrahepatic collections to steroid therapy as a surrogate marker for systemic inflammatory status
Defer colonoscopy until clinical stabilization due to high risk of iatrogenic complications in an unstable, edematous patient
